title: Infosys McCamish Enters Vermont Consent Order Over Cyber Incident Notice Allegations
detailed_summary: Infosys disclosed that Infosys McCamish Systems ('IMS'), a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, entered into a Stipulation and Consent Order with the State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation on July 9, 2025. The DFR alleged violations of the Vermont Security Breach Notice Act related to IMS's responses during the DFR's investigation into a cybersecurity event and alleged failures to provide timely and accurate notice to data owners. IMS resolved the matter without a hearing and without admitting the alleged violations. IMS is required to pay an administrative penalty of USD 125,000 within 30 calendar days of the order's entry.

key_facts.fact: On July 9, 2025, Infosys McCamish Systems ('IMS') and the State of Vermont Department of Financial Regulation entered into a Stipulation and Consent Order.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: IMS is a subsidiary of Infosys BPM limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of Infosys limited.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The DFR alleged violations of the Vermont Security Breach Notice Act by IMS relating to timely and accurate information, records, and notices connected to a cybersecurity event.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: The Stipulation and Consent Order resolves the matter without a hearing and specifically records that IMS does not admit the existence of the alleged violations.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident

key_facts.fact: Within 30 calendar days of the entry of the Stipulation and Consent Order by the Commissioner, IMS is required to pay an administrative penalty of USD 125,000.
key_facts.section_heading: Sub:  Update on McCamish Cyber Incident
